description_long: This humanoid robot features sleek, metallic blue legs with a streamlined, futuristic design, and white, blocky feet that suggest stability and durability. The legs are elongated and anatomically human-like, with subtle panel lines and joint segments visible at the knees and ankles, indicating advanced articulation. The feet are robust and rectangular, with a smooth, matte finish and minimal detailing, likely designed for urban navigation. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal or high-strength polymer, with a glossy blue coating on the legs and a matte white finish on the feet. The robot's lower body is engineered for both mobility and resilience, suitable for traversing sidewalks, streets, and other urban environments. No visible clothing, accessories, or external sensors are present on the legs or feet, emphasizing a utilitarian, high-tech aesthetic.
description: A humanoid robot with metallic blue, articulated legs and sturdy white feet, designed for urban mobility. The design is sleek, minimal, and futuristic.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated blue leg and the rectangular white foot, highlighting the knee and ankle joints.
description_view_1: Front view displaying both blue legs and white feet, emphasizing symmetry and the streamlined, human-like form.
description_view_2: Opposite side view of the blue leg and white foot, mirroring the features seen in view 0.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the backs of both blue legs and white feet, with visible heel structure and joint articulation.
attribute_pose: Standing upright with both legs straight and parallel, feet flat on the ground, and no visible movement or gesture.
attribute_clothing_style: No visible clothing; the robot has integrated blue panels on the legs and white coverings on the feet, all appearing as part of its structural design.
attribute_facial_features: No facial features are visible in these views; only the lower body is shown.
attribute_limb_articulation: Visible hinge joints at the knees and ankles, allowing for forward and backward movement; legs appear to have at least two major articulation points each, with rigid, mechanical construction.
